title: Chapter 9 Quiz
description: Test your mastery over Rust's file architecture and Cargo!

questions.question: What specifically is a Module in Rust?
options: A logical sandbox used to organize related functions and strictly control scope
A data type similar to an Enum
questions.explanation: Modules group code cleanly and manage exactly what is public or private.

questions.question: To correctly declare a module pointing natively to an external file named `network.rs`, you explicitly type `________ network;` in your main file.
questions.explanation: The `mod` keyword successfully tells Rust to seamlessly locate a file or folder explicitly matching that module name.

questions.question: By default natively, what exactly is the strict visibility natively of all functions securely declared inside a module?
questions.explanation: Rust prioritizes encapsulation securely cleanly locking everything down until explicitly strictly opened natively.

questions.question: To effectively permit external code to use a function, you must prefix it with the `pub` keyword.
questions.explanation: The `pub` keyword makes an item public.

questions.question: To natively cleanly cleanly successfully add an external package, you edit the `________.toml` file.
questions.explanation: Cargo optimally reliably uses the Cargo.toml file cleanly.
